channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Pandas vs Polars: Which One Should You Use in 2025?
item.description: Pandas vs Polars: Which One Should You Use in 2025? You’re probably still typing df.groupby() in Pandas while your Polars-using teammate has already finished their ETL pipeline and is on lunch....

item.title: ArborDb: a Rust document store where reading one field doesn't get slower as the record grows
item.description: Most embedded document stores make you pay the same price to read one field as to read the whole record: they deserialize the entire value, then hand you the field you asked for. That cost grows...

item.title: Scraping Indian government open data in 2026: what actually works
item.description: I maintain Village Finder, an open-source mapping project tracking over 78,000 Indian villages. It works by pulling daily raw updates straight from official public administration feeds. That means...

item.title: Mexico’s PSM registry after CTA: what filings still matter
item.description: Mexico’s Public Registry of Commerce still runs through state-level implementation. Look up the same company twice and historical coverage can shift depending on which Registro Público de Comercio...

item.title: The Vibe Coding Hangover, Part 2: Your AI Didn't Just Write Bugs, It Wrote Backdoors
item.description: Back in January, I wrote about the vibe coding hangover. The morning-after feeling of shipping fast with AI, then realizing nobody actually understands the codebase anymore, least of all the...

item.title: Stop Using 6 Chrome Tabs for Code Reviews—Do It in Your Terminal
item.description: You know that moment when you're reviewing a PR and you need to: Check the GitHub diff (tab 1) Search stack overflow for the pattern (tab 2) Open the docs (tab 3) Look at existing similar code...

item.title: Even Nvidia’s head of automotive fights with Nvidia for compute
item.description: Today, I’m talking with Xinzhou Wu, who is the head of automotive at Nvidia. Nvidia is obviously in the news constantly because of the AI boom — it’s one of the most valuable companies in the...

item.title: Isn't Just Only a Term (Tech Winter)
item.description: Should I quit IT or just live through the burnout? Low-effort AI content as a burnout trigger Klaudia Grzondziel Klaudia Grzondziel Klaudia Grzondziel Follow Jul 9 Should I quit IT or just live...

item.title: [Boost]
item.description: I created a platform with a list to help you share your projects 🔥 Anthony Max Anthony Max Anthony Max Follow Jul 13 I created a platform with a list to help you share your projects 🔥 #webdev...

item.title: Indian villages with live market prices, government schemes, and soil data
item.description: I mapped 78,000+ Indian villages with live market prices, government schemes, and soil data (Serverless) If you've ever tried to query rural geographic data in India, you know the workflow:...

item.title: The art and engineering of Sega CD Silpheed

item.title: The Asus ROG Flow Z13 gaming tablet with 64GB RAM is down to $2,100
item.description: Almost all gaming laptops have gone up in price over the past year. Curiously, the surprisingly fast Asus ROG Flow Z13 gaming laptop/tablet combo has not. It’s actually gotten quite a bit cheaper...

item.title: Waze adds new AI-powered features and customization updates
item.description: Some of the new features are powered by Google's Gemini AI assistant, which reflects the the tech giant's broader push to integrate Gemini across its products while also better positioning Waze to...

item.title: Forg365 PhaaS Targets Microsoft 365 with Device Code and AitM Session Theft
item.description: A new phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) operation called Forg365 is using a combination of device code phishing, adversary-in-the-middle (AitM) tactics, antibot evasion, artificial intelligence...

item.title: The Download: a donor conception cap and world models for AI
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Sperm donors need limits, says a European fertility group...

item.title: Meta Files Patent for AI That Can Listen All Day and Track How You're Feeling
item.description: Meta has filed a patent application for an AI that listens to your voice throughout the day, works out how it thinks you are feeling from the way you sound, and keeps a timestamped log of every...

item.title: Thinking Fast and Slow in the SOC: The Case for Combining Autonomous AI with Analyst Copilots
item.description: A few days ago, I was sitting with the CISO of a Fortune 50 company, walking through how his security team was thinking about AI agents in the SOC. Smart team. Serious program. They had already...

item.title: Attacker Uses Suspected AI-Generated PowerShell Script to Map Active Directory
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged an intrusion in which an unknown threat actor leveraged a vibe-coded PowerShell script for Active Directory (AD) enumeration. "The script looked for the...

item.title: AI Data Centers and the Concentration of Wealth
item.description: This essay was written with Nathan E. Sanders, and originally appeared in The Guardian. Opposition to AI data centers has emerged as a primary theme in US politics, one that—surprisingly—doesn’t...

item.title: This free Mac app reveals the truth about your mystery USB-C cables
item.description: Nearly three years ago, I showed you an awesome $8 cable tester that quickly tells you if your USB-C cable is likely fast, slow, powerful, or weak. Sadly, that gadget got discontinued, and I've...

item.title: Social media limits are coming for teens across Europe
item.description: The European Union is weighing sweeping new restrictions on children's and teenagers' access to social media, including age limits, an outright ban, and phased access. Social media platforms could...

item.title: Waze is getting a bunch of new AI-powered features
item.description: Waze is getting an AI makeover. Google is integrating its flagship AI assistant, Gemini, into the driving app with the goal of letting users personalize their trips a little more. Of the four new...

item.title: Misconfigured Server Reveals Three Evilginx Phishing Operations Targeting Microsoft 365
item.description: An attacker running a live Microsoft 365 phishing operation left a Python web server listening on a public port with directory listing switched on. The command that did it: python3 -m http.server...

item.title: iCagenda and Balbooa Forms Joomla Flaws Reportedly Exploited as Zero-Days
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added two maximum-severity security flaws impacting iCagenda and Balbooa extensions for Joomla to its Known Exploited...

item.title: Lorde says Ray-Ban Meta AI glasses are ‘not sexy’
item.description: Lorde was performing at the Real Cool Festival in Madrid on Thursday and took some time during her set to speak out against AI glasses. While she didn't specify any brands in particular, it's...

item.title: Shall We Go On Sinning So That Grace May Increase? is hypnotic, healing, and hopeful
item.description: Matmos are an incredibly accomplished duo between their own solo records like the masterpiece A Chance to Cut Is a Chance to Cure and production classic Björk records like Vespertine. But Drew...

item.title: Apple’s failed self-driving car program left a legacy of powerful AI chips
item.description: Apple's self-driving car program never really got off the ground, but it may have been what made the company's chips the powerful AI performers they are. Early in the development of the...

item.title: One of SteelSeries’ best gaming headsets is over $100 off
item.description: SteelSeries has the Arctis Nova Pro Wireless gaming headset on sale for $239.99 (currently between $300 and $350 at other retailers). The Xbox version that supports a host of other platforms...

item.title: How Philips Hue got the smart home right
item.description: The state of the smart home can be frustrating, because it is just so obvious how things ought to work. You should be able to control everything from everywhere. Your spaces should adapt to what...

item.title: Scientists’ Side Hustle? Using AI and Quantum Computing to Generate New Peptides
item.description: Researchers cobbled together funding and time to show how quantum computing could aid in the development of drugs to help underserved populations and combat rare diseases.
